gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/137)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/137)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ أَصْحَابَ الْمُغِيرَةِ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنْ أَكْلِ الْقَدِيدِ الَّتِي لَمْ تَمَسَّهُ النَّارُ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِأَكْلِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Biin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Abdul Sammad Bin Basheer, from Atiyya a brother of Abu Al Magra who said,

‘I said to Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The companions of Al-Mugheira are forbidding from eating the Jerk which the fire has not touched’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with eating it’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Hassan ibn Ali from ‘Abd al-Samad ibn Bashir from ‘ Atiyyah brother of al-Mighra’ who has said the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that companions of al-Mughirah prohibit eating of meat which is dried up in the sun and fire has not touched it. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not unlawful to eat it.’”
